children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Administrator of the Small Business Administration (referred to in this subtitle as the “Administrator”) shall establish a Small Business Manufacturing Task Force (referred to in this section as the “Task Force”) to address the concerns of small manufacturers.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Chair
children.label: b Chair
children.text: The Administrator shall assign a member of the Task Force to serve as chair of the Task Force.
evaluate and identify whether programs and services are sufficient to serve the needs of small manufacturers;
actively promote the programs and services of the Small Business Administration that serve small manufacturers; and
identify and study the unique conditions facing small manufacturers and develop and propose policy initiatives to support and assist small manufacturers.

children.heading: Frequency
children.label: 1 Frequency
children.text: The Task Force shall meet not less than 4 times per year, and more frequently if necessary to perform its duties.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 2 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the members of the Task Force shall constitute a quorum to approve recommendations or reports.

children.heading: Compensation of members
children.label: 1 Compensation of members
children.text: Each member of the Task Force shall serve without compensation in addition to that received for services rendered as an officer or employee of the United States.

children.heading: Detail of SBA employees
children.label: 2 Detail of SBA employees
children.text: Any employee of the Small Business Administration may be detailed to the Task Force without reimbursement, and such detail shall be without interruption or loss of civil service status or privilege.
the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship of the Senate; and
the Committee on Small Business of the House of Representatives.
